page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.medium is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. This single-core, single-threaded arm64 CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz and features a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. The underlying virtualization is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate moderate single-core capabilities alongside expected multi-core limitations due to the single-vCPU configuration. The processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1694.58 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1741.0, both falling within average performance tiers. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable strength, reaching 14.0 GB/sec. However, memory-intensive operations show constraints, with a large-block read bandwidth of 30.06 GB/sec and a write bandwidth of 11.68 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also relatively low, with Redis processing 2.45 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gd.medium offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring a high memory-to-core ratio and fast local scratch space without the overhead of multi-core licensing. The inclusion of a 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store provides high-speed local storage directly attached to the instance. This configuration is best suited for lightweight memory-bound applications, single-threaded data processing, caching layers, and small-scale database workloads that benefit from local SSD storage and a dedicated ARMv9 core.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.medium is a memory-optimized, single-core virtual machine powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this arm64 instance features 1 dedicated vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 1741.0 and a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.0 GB/sec, though memory bandwidth and multi-core throughput are naturally constrained by the single-vCPU architecture. This instance provides a cost-efficient, memory-dense profile with fast local storage, making it highly suitable for lightweight database operations, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks.

8.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
